## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

XTB (full name: **X-Trade Brokers Dom Maklerski S.A.**) was founded in **2002** in **Warsaw, Poland** by **Jakub Zabłocki** (born 1975), an economics graduate from the University of Łódź and former mBank employee. Zabłocki launched the company with just **300,000 Polish złoty (PLN)** — approximately $75,000 at the time.

On **November 21, 2005**, XTB received its brokerage license from the **Polish Financial Supervision Authority (KNF)**, enabling it to offer services for all financial instruments. In 2007, the company began its international expansion, opening branches in the Czech Republic, followed by Germany, Spain, Romania, Slovakia, France, Portugal, Italy, and Hungary.

In **2009**, the company rebranded as **XTB Online Trading**. By 2010, XTB had established itself as the **largest forex and CFD brokerage in Central and Eastern Europe**, with net profits exceeding €12 million. That year, Enterprise Investors acquired a minority stake for **€57 million**.

XTB completed its IPO on the **Warsaw Stock Exchange (WSE)** in **May 2016**, achieving a valuation of approximately **1.8 billion PLN**. At the time, founder Zabłocki held 66% of the company.

The company experienced explosive growth during the COVID-19 pandemic, reporting record quarterly revenue of **$79 million (307 million PLN)** in Q1 2020 — surpassing its entire 2019 revenue. By 2022, XTB reported its most profitable year with net profit of **$174 million**.

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

XTB (full name: **X-Trade Brokers Dom Maklerski S.A.**) was founded in **2002** in **Warsaw, Poland** by **Jakub Zabłocki** (born 1975), an economics graduate from the University of Łódź and former mBank employee. Zabłocki launched the company with just **300,000 Polish złoty (PLN)** — approximately $75,000 at the time.

On **November 21, 2005**, XTB received its brokerage license from the **Polish Financial Supervision Authority (KNF)**, enabling it to offer services for all financial instruments. In 2007, the company began its international expansion, opening branches in the Czech Republic, followed by Germany, Spain, Romania, Slovakia, France, Portugal, Italy, and Hungary.

In **2009**, the company rebranded as **XTB Online Trading**. By 2010, XTB had established itself as the **largest forex and CFD brokerage in Central and Eastern Europe**, with net profits exceeding €12 million. That year, Enterprise Investors acquired a minority stake for **€57 million**.

XTB completed its IPO on the **Warsaw Stock Exchange (WSE)** in **May 2016**, achieving a valuation of approximately **1.8 billion PLN**. At the time, founder Zabłocki held 66% of the company.

The company experienced explosive growth during the COVID-19 pandemic, reporting record quarterly revenue of **$79 million (307 million PLN)** in Q1 2020 — surpassing its entire 2019 revenue. By 2022, XTB reported its most profitable year with net profit of **$174 million**.
